MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Set For Trial On Mar-30-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Jul-16-2025 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ELDA GONZALEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to compel further responses and for sanctions is denied. This court previously ordered the parties to meet and confer and put the motion over for further hearing. The court set a supplemental briefing schedule. Plaintiff has not filed a supplemental brief; defendant has filed a supplemental brief detailing its supplemental responses. Because Plaintiff has not identified any remaining deficiencies in Ford's responses, the motion is denied.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the court's ruling, with a cover letter demonstrating compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)

Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff´S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Continued To Jul-16-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 301
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PLAINTIFF ELDA GONZALEZ's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff Elda Gonzalez's motion to compel further responses to her request for production of documents, set one, from Ford Motor Company is continued on the court's own motion to July 16, 2025.

The court finds that Gonzalez did not sufficiently meet and confer before bringing this motion. Gonzalez sent a meet and confer letter to Ford that attached two sample discovery orders. On January 15, 2025, Ford offered to produce the documents outlined in those sample orders, subjective to a protective order, and offered a time to confer telephonically. (Tully Dec. para. 6 & Ex. 3.) Gonzalez did not respond to Ford's letter, other than to sign the proposed protective order that was enclosed, and instead moved to compel without any further efforts to narrow the dispute.

The court orders Gonzalez to meet and confer telephonically or in person concerning the compromise Ford offered in its letter of January 15. The court is hopeful that the parties will resolve their outstanding discovery disputes. The motion is continued to July 16 unless it is withdrawn by Gonzalez in the interim. Gonzalez shall file a supplemental brief no later than nine court days in advance of the continued hearing date concerning what issues are still in dispute. She must also file an updated separate statement that shows specific facts and good cause to order production of any documents that remain in dispute. Ford may respond by supplemental brief and updated separate statement by no later than five court days in advance of the hearing.

CCP 2031.300(c) provides that "the court shall impose a monetary sanction . . . against any party, person, or attorney who unsuccessfully makes or opposes a motion to compel a response to a demand for inspection . . . unless it finds that the one subject to the sanction acted with substantial justification or that other circumstances make the imposition of the sanction unjust." The failure to meet and confer is a misuse of the discovery process and burdens the court with reviewing disputes that the parties could resolve through good-faith efforts. (CCP 2023.010(i).) The court finds that Gonzalez acted without substantial justification in failing to meet and confer and orders her to pay sanctions of $500 to the San Francisco Superior Court within 10 court days of entry of this order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, with a cover letter reflecting compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
